scenarios.text: Open the recording from yesterday and summarize the main action items.
Set a reminder for nine in the morning to review the budget spreadsheet.
scenarios.note: Inworld stochastically normalises 'nine in the morning' to either '9' (1 substitution, WER 0.077) or '9:00' (1 substitution + 1 insertion via colon-split, WER 0.154). Both are semantically correct time-context normalisation. Threshold 0.20 covers natural variance while still catching real degradation.

scenarios.text: Schedule a quick sync with the design team this afternoon.
Открой последнюю запись и покажи краткое содержание главных пунктов.
scenarios.note: Russian imperative-vs-future-tense phonetic ambiguity: 'Открой/покажи' (imperative) sounds nearly identical to 'Открою/покажу' (1st-person future) when spoken without stress. Observed WER 0.000-0.222 across iterations. Threshold 0.30 covers natural variance while still catching real model breakage.

scenarios.text: Найди все встречи на следующей неделе с командой разработки.

When we were planning the next quarter we agreed on three top priorities. First, we should ship the redesigned settings page by the end of May, including the new privacy controls. Second, we need to finish the migration to the v2 transcription pipeline, which will reduce latency for live dictation. Third, we want to onboard at least five enterprise pilot customers before the team retreat in June.
scenarios.note: Tightened back from 0.18 -> 0.10 after merge_finals() now reconstructs proper word boundaries from Inworld's leading-space-marked finals. Observed WER dropped from 0.075-0.134 (naive join) to ~0.015 (smart merge). Threshold 0.10 catches real degradation while accepting normal STT variance.

scenarios.text: Email me at hello at example dot com or call four one five five five five one two three four. The deadline is March third twenty twenty six.
scenarios.note: Inworld auto-normalises spoken digits/punct: 'four one five' -> '415', 'at' -> '@', 'dot' -> '.', 'twenty twenty six' -> '2026'. Output is semantically correct but mismatches naive word-token WER. Stable observed WER 0.571. Threshold 0.65 catches catastrophic regression (transcript drops to empty -> WER 1.0). TODO: replace expected text with the digit-form Inworld actually returns; this also tests whether Inworld's text-normalisation pipeline still works.

scenarios.text: Add a new function called process underscore audio that takes a buffer and returns a transcript object.
Schedule a meeting with Mikhail Wiseman and the WaiSay team in San Francisco next Tuesday.
Send a private message to the founder about the demo tomorrow.
Save.
Um, so, like, send the email to John tomorrow morning.
I want to to send a message about the new feature.
Go to github dot com slash anthropic slash claude code.
scenarios.note: Inworld returns spoken-form URLs verbatim ('github dot com slash anthropic') instead of normalising to 'github.com/anthropic'. Naive token WER counts each word as substitution. Observed 0.300-0.600 across iterations. Threshold 0.65 catches catastrophic failure (transcript drops to empty -> WER 1.0). TODO: pair with a cleanup-tier scenario whose cleaned_expected uses the URL form to test that the Anthropic cleanup prompt or a future spoken-URL normaliser handles it.

scenarios.text: Schedule O'Brien's meeting for next Friday afternoon.
Run npm install with the save dev flag and then run the test suite.
Schedule a follow up for the patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
Um so like send the email to John about the meeting tomorrow morning.
scenarios.cleaned_expected: Send the email to John about the meeting tomorrow morning.
scenarios.note: Tier 1.5: STT preserves fillers verbatim, then Anthropic /cleanup must strip 'um, so, like'. clean_wer compares post-cleanup output against cleaned_expected to detect prompt drift in backend/app/api/routes/dictation.py.

scenarios.text: I want to to send a message about the new feature.
scenarios.cleaned_expected: I want to send a message about the new feature.
scenarios.note: Tier 1.5: STT keeps the 'to to' stutter, cleanup must drop the false-start per the prompt's example pattern.

scenarios.text: Ну, типа, отправь сообщение Ивану о встрече завтра утром.
scenarios.cleaned_expected: Отправь сообщение Ивану о встрече завтра утром.
scenarios.note: Tier 1.5 Russian: cleanup prompt explicitly lists 'ну, вот, типа, как бы, значит' as Russian fillers to strip. This scenario verifies the RU branch of the prompt actually fires (previously only EN cleanup was covered).
